adjudication.reason: The section text requires at least reasonable care and no less than the care used for its own confidential information of similar importance, matching the fallback exactly in substance. The additional restriction on intentional disclosure makes it more protective, not less.

items.prompt:
Redline Gym answer-key defect adjudication.
Given the rule position, fallback, and the contract section text, decide whether the section contains a GENUINE violation of the rule. Treat text that matches or is more protective than the fallback as compliant. Return STRICT JSON only: {"genuine": true|false, "reason": "..."}.

Rule R-005 position:
The care standard must be at least reasonable care and no less than the recipient uses for its own similar confidential information.

Fallback:
Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care and no less than the care it uses to protect its own similar confidential information.

Section text:
## 4. Protection Standard

Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care, and in no event less than the degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance. Recipient will not intentionally disclose it except as permitted by this Agreement.
items.quote: Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care, and in no event less than the degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance.

The additional restriction on intentional disclosure makes it more protective, not less."}
items.section_text:
## 4. Protection Standard

Recipient will protect Confidential Information using at least reasonable care, and in no event less than the degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information of similar importance. Recipient will not intentionally disclose it except as permitted by this Agreement.

adjudication.reason: The section requires prompt notice and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment, matching the rule position. Though it lacks the explicit 'before any compelled disclosure' phrasing of the fallback, the obligation to provide notice upon being required to disclose, combined with the duty to cooperate in seeking protective treatment, functionally ensures prior notice and is equally protective.

items.prompt:
Redline Gym answer-key defect adjudication.
Given the rule position, fallback, and the contract section text, decide whether the section contains a GENUINE violation of the rule. Treat text that matches or is more protective than the fallback as compliant. Return STRICT JSON only: {"genuine": true|false, "reason": "..."}.

Rule R-007 position:
Compelled disclosure should require prompt notice where legally permitted and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment.

Fallback:
Before any compelled disclosure, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice and reasonable cooperation so Discloser may seek protective treatment.

Section text:
## 5. Compelled Disclosure

If Recipient is required by law, regulation, subpoena, court order, or governmental request to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice to Discloser and reasonably cooperate with Discloser in seeking confidential or protective treatment.
items.quote: If Recipient is required by law, regulation, subpoena, court order, or governmental request to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice to Discloser and reasonably cooperate with Discloser in seeking confidential or protective treatment.
items.raw_response: {"genuine": false, "reason": "The section requires prompt notice and reasonable cooperation to seek protective treatment, matching the rule position. Though it lacks the explicit 'before any compelled disclosure' phrasing of the fallback, the obligation to provide notice upon being required to disclose, combined with the duty to cooperate in seeking protective treatment, functionally ensures prior notice and is equally protective."}
items.section_text:
## 5. Compelled Disclosure

If Recipient is required by law, regulation, subpoena, court order, or governmental request to disclose Confidential Information, Recipient will, to the extent legally permitted, provide prompt notice to Discloser and reasonably cooperate with Discloser in seeking confidential or protective treatment.

adjudication.reason: The section contains a residual knowledge clause in violation of the rule that such clauses must be absent unless expressly approved by the client. The clause states it is only effective if 'the parties approve that right in a signed writing,' but the clause itself is present without the required client approval. The fallback requires deletion of the clause unless the client expressly approves it in writing.

Rule R-012 position:
Residual knowledge clauses should be absent unless expressly approved by the client.

Fallback:
Delete the residual knowledge clause unless expressly approved by the client in writing.

Section text:
## 12. Residual Rights

No right to use residual knowledge, unaided memory, or retained impressions is granted under this Agreement unless the parties approve that right in a signed writing.

## 12. Residual Rights

adjudication.reason: The section defines 'Purpose' identically to the fallback language, which includes performing the transaction, and is therefore compliant with the rule.

items.prompt:
Redline Gym answer-key defect adjudication.
Given the rule position, fallback, and the contract section text, decide whether the section contains a GENUINE violation of the rule. Treat text that matches or is more protective than the fallback as compliant. Return STRICT JSON only: {"genuine": true|false, "reason": "..."}.

Rule R-004 position:
Use should be limited to evaluating and negotiating the contemplated transaction.

Fallback:
The recipient may use Confidential Information solely to evaluate, negotiate, and, if agreed, perform the contemplated transaction between the parties.

Section text:
## 1. Confidential Information

"Confidential Information" means information disclosed by or on behalf of a Discloser that bears a restricted legend when provided or is later identified in a written follow-up delivered within five days after disclosure. "Purpose" means evaluating, negotiating, and, if agreed, performing the contemplated AI workflow pilot between the parties. Confidential Information includes written, electronic, oral, visual, technical, financial, product, customer, operational, and business information.

Rule R-012 position:
Residual knowledge clauses should be absent unless expressly approved by the client.

Fallback:

Section text:
## 10. No License; Ownership

Disclosure does not transfer title to a party's materials. The receiving side may keep and apply generalized ideas, techniques, and know-how drawn from the other side's materials in later work.
